title: selinum protractum
intro: Selinum protractum, also known as Ashwagandha, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Apiaceae and is native to India, Africa, and the Middle East. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly in Ayurvedic practices, for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and adaptogenic properties. The health benefits of Ashwagandha include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and enhancing cognitive function, as well as supporting immune function and exercise recovery. Ashwagandha can be prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or powder, and is also used in traditional medicine as a paste or oil.
